div.p:
Mr. Crowley, Walter ThayerWalter Thayer, General Counsel of Foreign Economic Administration. and I had a meeting this afternoon with Secretary PattersonRobert P. Patterson, Secretary of War. and General Somervell,Gen. Brehon Somervell, Commanding General, Army Service Forces. and the following decisions were made: 1.
Reverse lend-lease ceases on the same day that lend-lease ceases. However, for any services such as rent, transportation, etc., furnished us by lend-lease countries after September 2, payment should be made by giving them credit on their 3(c) obligations or in surplus property. 2.
We are to tell Belgium that we recognize the obligation to balance the lend-lease account with them and will do so in some way, probably by delivery of surplus property.
